title: SICP smoke harness — 6 canonical exercises as ICC oracle probes
goal: Add 6 canonical SICP exercises as runnable Eshkol files + corresponding ICC smoke probes. Once they all pass, 'Eshkol reproduces SICP' becomes a mechanically-checkable claim. The 6 are deliberately chosen to stress the language features SICP relies on most heavily.
next_actions: tests/v1_3_edge_cases/sicp/sieve_of_eratosthenes_test.esk: §3.5.2. (require core.sicp-streams). Define sieve via stream-filter recursion. Assert (stream-ref primes 50) == 233.
tests/v1_3_edge_cases/sicp/metacircular_eval_test.esk: §4.1. Copy SICP's eval+apply+env code verbatim. Assert (eval '(+ 1 2) the-global-environment) inside the metacircular evaluator returns 3.
tests/v1_3_edge_cases/sicp/lazy_evaluator_test.esk: §4.2. SICP's lazy interpreter. Assert (try (* 1 (/ 1 0)) 5) returns 5 (lazy short-circuit).
tests/v1_3_edge_cases/sicp/amb_multiple_dwelling_test.esk: §4.3. SICP's CPS amb evaluator. Assert (multiple-dwelling) produces ((baker 3) (cooper 2) (fletcher 4) (miller 5) (smith 1)).
tests/v1_3_edge_cases/sicp/query_same_supervisor_test.esk: §4.4. SICP's query system on a small KB. Assert that (job ?x (computer programmer)) over Eva Lu Ator's company KB returns the expected set.
tests/v1_3_edge_cases/sicp/register_machine_fib_test.esk: §5.2. SICP's register-machine simulator running iterative-fib for n=10. Assert result == 55.
Add a `sicp_smoke_complete` ICC probe in scripts/run_icc_smoke.sh that runs all 6 and exits 0 only if all pass.
Add a `sicp-reproduces` ICC oracle in .icc/completion-oracles.yaml gated on the smoke probe.
acceptance: All 6 SICP exercise files compile and produce the expected output.
Each test prints PASS/FAIL on its own; the harness rolls them up.
ICC oracle `sicp-reproduces` flips to complete with all 6 probes PASS.
verification: for t in tests/v1_3_edge_cases/sicp/*.esk; do ./build/eshkol-run $t -o /tmp/sicp_test && /tmp/sicp_test || exit 1; done
ux_payoff: Turns 'we can reproduce SICP in Eshkol' from a claim into a verifiable property of every commit.
